summary: Comprehensive testing of per-tenant WhatsApp credentials feature (backend + frontend). 22/22 new backend tests PASS including cross-tenant isolation, verify_token uniqueness, X-Hub-Signature-256 enforcement (signed accept + unsigned reject when app_secret set + unsigned accept when no app_secret), inbound message tagging with correct company_id, secret masking (raw token never returned), SA 400 restrictions on all tenant endpoints, partial-update preserves secrets, delete + company-cascade removes wa config. Frontend UI validated for both Company Admin flow (empty state → save → Step 1 webhook card with Callback URL + Verify Token starting 'tzs_' → Step 3 quicksend → Delete → back to Not configured) and Super Admin platform overview (tenant list, sidebar 'WhatsApp Setup' correctly hidden for SA, visible for tenant admin, platform-only items /providers /webhooks /companies hidden for tenant admin). P0 isolation regression (iteration 8): 27/28 pass — 1 transient network timeout on /reminders/upcoming (not a code regression).

issues: Recharts warning: 'width(-1) height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' — container sizing issue on some charts. Pre-existing, not introduced by this iteration.

action_items: Optional: Investigate pre-existing Recharts container sizing warnings on Dashboard (unrelated to this iteration).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2218 lines — recommend splitting per-feature routers (whatsapp/, webhook/, companies/, contacts/, etc.) to improve maintainability. Not blocking.
GOOD: raw access_token / app_secret are never returned by any endpoint — only *_preview (masked last-4) and *_set boolean. Verified explicitly.
GOOD: verify_token is per-tenant and unique (secrets.token_urlsafe(24), 'tzs_' prefix).
GOOD: When app_secret is set, tenant webhook enforces X-Hub-Signature-256 (401 on invalid); when not set, signature check is bypassed — consistent with legacy behaviour.
GOOD: Company delete correctly cascades and removes company_whatsapp_configs row (verified by SA tenant_count drop and tenant list filter).
GOOD: cflt/scoping applied to messages/contacts created via inbound tenant webhook (verified: A cannot see B's inbound contact).

MINOR: PUT accepts empty strings for access_token — currently spec says empty means 'leave unchanged' (frontend strips before send). Backend `if v is not None` allows empty strings to overwrite. Frontend guards this correctly. Could tighten backend to also skip empty strings for safety.

success_rate.backend: 100% (22/22 new; 27/28 P0 regression — 1 transient net timeout)

test_credentials: SA admin@cpaas.io / Admin@12345. Ephemeral tenants created per test run with TEST_ prefix; auto-cleaned via company delete cascade.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral: 3 tenants (watest_A+*, watest_B+*, watest_cascade+*) plus 1 UI tenant (watest_UI+1784032793@test.com) created during tests; all cleaned via /api/companies DELETE cascade.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature is fully verified. Reuse test_iteration9_wa_pertenant.py for regression. All tenant-scoped WhatsApp behaviours (config CRUD, masking, per-tenant verify_token, tenant webhook GET+POST with signature enforcement, cross-tenant isolation, cascade) are covered. If retesting P0 isolation, note the reminders/upcoming timeout is intermittent — retry once.
